Music
is your
fingerprint.

No two people on earth play the same songs, in the same order, on the same nights. Your listening history is the most individual signature you produce. Sonar reads it — and finds the one or two people whose fingerprint answers yours.

experience the demo why music
scroll
why music

Your taste is a fingerprint.
Yours alone.

No two people on earth play the same songs, in the same order, on the same nights. The rhythms you return to, the lyrics you memorize without trying, the tracks you put on alone at 2am — those are not preferences. They are identity at its highest possible resolution.

Every person is singular. Every person carries a signature no one else produces. For most of human history there was no way to read that signature at scale — the data didn't exist, and the tools to compute it didn't exist.

That changed. Streaming created the raw material — a decade of honest listening history for a billion people. Modern AI makes that material legible. A globally connected internet means the match doesn't have to live down the street. For the first time, identity can be read at the resolution of the individual — and matched across the whole world.

This is what Sonar is: the greatest compatibility technology humans have ever had — used first to help you understand yourself, and then to find the one or two people whose fingerprint answers your own.

the crisis

We've never been more connected.
Or more alone.

The technology to read individual identity finally exists. The reason we need it now has never been more urgent. These are not separate statistics — they are one story, told eight different ways.

52%
of Americans report feeling lonely — classified as a public health epidemic
Cigna U.S. Loneliness Index, 2021
13%
of Gen Z have no close friends at all. The loneliest generation in recorded history.
Survey Center on American Life, 2023
↓63%
decline in marriage rate since 1970. People cannot find their person.
CDC National Center for Health Statistics
↓20%
birth rate decline since 2007. The downstream consequence of disconnection.
CDC National Center for Health Statistics
↓50%
of Americans with a close friend group — down from 1990.
Survey Center on American Life, 2021
↓29%
of young adults in romantic relationships compared to prior generations.
Pew Research Center
57%
of dating app users describe their experience as negative. The apps are making it worse.
Pew Research Center, 2023
↑31%
increase in depression among young adults since 2012 — the year smartphones went mainstream.
National Institute of Mental Health
"We built the most connected infrastructure in human history — and used it to make people feel more alone. The tools failed not because of bad execution. They failed because they measured the wrong thing."
why current solutions fail

No more swipe left.
No more swipe right.
On a photo.

🔥
Tinder
↓23%
downloads from peak. Built entirely on photos.
🐝
Bumble
↓80%
stock from IPO high. Revenue growth stalled.
💎
Match Group
↓70%
stock from 2021 high. Owns Tinder, Hinge, OkCupid.
👥
Bumble BFF
~0%
retention after 90 days. Friendship cold start unsolved.

Every platform measures self-presentation — the curated, performed, audience-aware version of a person. Self-presentation is the least predictive variable for genuine compatibility that exists. We need a signal that bypasses performance entirely.

compatibility in motion

Two signatures meet.
Recognition.

Before any word, any photo, any profile — Sonar compares listening identity and surfaces the people who already share your inner frequency.

introducing sonar

We share our music.
Our DNA. Our soul.

Your listening history is the most honest data point a platform has ever had access to. Not what you say you like — what you demonstrably play, alone, at 2am.

your sonic signature · always transmitting

how it works
1
🎵
Sonar reads. Not asks.
Connect Spotify or Apple Music. Sonar analyzes your actual listening history — weighted by recency, repetition, and rarity. The truth about who you are, written in songs.
2
🧬
Your Sonic DNA.
47 acoustic dimensions. Emotional texture. Cognitive style. Energy signature. Listening context. This is your identity — not how you describe yourself, but how music describes you.
3
🌊
Recognition. Not introduction.
People near you ranked by real compatibility. When you find your unicorn match — a 0.4% shared track neither of you knew the other listened to — the first message doesn't feel like meeting. It feels like remembering.
the unicorn principle

Not taste overlap.
Recognition.

Two people who both listen to Drake share taste. Two people who both play an obscure track with 4,000 monthly listeners on obsessive rotation share something exponentially deeper — an independent discovery, a private resonance, identical inner worlds.

Sonar weights rare shared songs exponentially higher than common ones. A unicorn match changes everything.

The musical equivalent of finishing each other's sentences before you've ever spoken.

the science

20 years of research.
One conclusion.

Five independent peer-reviewed studies. Over 10,000 participants. All pointing to the same signal.

01
Music is the #1 getting-acquainted topic — across all cultures
Strangers have always known intuitively that music reveals identity. Before algorithms, they asked each other what they listened to.
Cambridge · Rentfrow & Gosling · Psychological Science · 2006
02
Accurate personality impressions form from playlists alone
No photo. No name. No demographics. Just a playlist — and observers accurately infer deep personality. The signal is that powerful.
Cambridge · Rentfrow & Gosling · 2006
03
Cognitive style predicts music taste better than personality itself
How your mind is built — systemizing vs. empathizing — determines what you listen to more than any personality trait measured.
Cambridge / Stanford · Greenberg et al. · PLOS ONE · 2015
04
Shared music creates bonds through shared values
Outlasts personality similarity as a predictor of attraction. Music is not the signal of taste. It is the signal of who you are.
Tilburg · Boer et al. · PSPB · 2011
05
Music preferences predict friendship stability over time
Longitudinally valid. Predicts not just the first conversation — but which relationships endure months and years later.
Radboud · Selfhout et al. · Journal of Adolescence · 2009
∞
700M users. The data exists. Nobody connected it.
Streaming music has existed at scale since 2016. The average user has 8+ years of private listening history. The signal has never been this rich. The timing is now.
Platform data · 2024

Why now.

Category-defining products require a specific convergence. Three conditions — all true simultaneously for the first time in history.

The data exists. 8+ years of continuous private listening for 700M users. Before streaming, this didn't exist.
The APIs are open. Spotify Web API and Apple MusicKit provide full listening history with consent. The pipes are live.
The competition has visibly failed. The market isn't saturated — it's disillusioned. A disillusioned market is waiting for something that works.
the founder

This product comes
from a specific
kind of pain.

"There was a period where I was severely depressed and suicidal. I couldn't find real connection. The thing that got me through it wasn't an app. It was music. Specific songs that made me feel less alone."

Years later I started asking why music does that. I found the science. And I realized: for the first time in history, that signal is digitized at scale — 700 million people generating the most honest identity dataset ever created. Nobody had built the connection layer on top of it.

I have 9 years of enterprise sales experience generating over $8M in revenue. I know how to build pipeline and close. But more than that — I know what it feels like to need something that doesn't exist yet. Sonar is not a feature I identified in a market analysis. It is the product that should have existed when I needed it most.

🇦🇫 Afghan-Canadian 📍 New York $8M+ revenue generated YC Summer 2026
